title: Malty Dense Ukrainian Paska Bake
description: Nostalgic Ukrainian dessert featuring raisins and a rich, malty flavor, perfect for special occasions.

ingredients.name: cocoa powder
ingredients.notes: replaces some of the flour for a deeper flavor

instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit and grease a 9x5-inch loaf pan.
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, sugar, and yeast.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the honey, water, and softened butter until well combined.
Add the egg mixture to the dry ingredients and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 10 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with plastic wrap, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until it has doubled in size.
Punch down the dough and add the raisins, cocoa powder, espresso powder, and salt. Mix until the raisins are evenly distributed.
Transfer the dough to the prepared loaf pan and cover it with plastic wrap.
Bake the paska for 45-50 minutes, or until it is golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped.
Remove the paska from the oven and let it cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer it to a wire rack to cool completely.
Once the paska has cooled, use a serrated knife to slice it into thick slices.
Serve the paska warm or at room temperature, dust with powdered sugar if desired.
To make the paska more egg-free friendly, omit the eggs and increase the water slightly to compensate for the lack of moisture.
Alternatively, you can replace the eggs with flaxseed or mashed banana to create a vegan version of this recipe.
